item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I might be missing on how this SCP connects to the [REDACT] in the hidden text.

Gotta re-read some of the connects SCPs to see if they have a connection with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just noticed this today, but that first image (5000-1.jpg) is 4.23 MB. That's going to take awhile to load on mobile devices, and might be a deterrent for people with limited data plans. Any chance that can be shrunk down?

ETA: feel free to poke me if you'd like assistance with resizing the image. Please send me a wikidot PM if I don't see a reply to this post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is fair as the punchline/ending of this was what I had the most trouble with. The design process in writing as part of a team contest, then linking it to the other scenario, then making it a standalone article was a problem. However, I don't quite agree there's no distinct anomaly here - I did envision something distinct going on in here beyond it just being 3848 events happening. Thanks for reading, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's definitely thematic similarities, but it wasn't something I had that strongly in mind when writing it. Personally I think 3000 is a very 'force of nature' kind of thing, whereas this is similar-but-opposite in being more about forgotten history and human stories. Glad you enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to admit a certain disappointment. The punchline to this seems to be a completely different SCP. Like, there's not an actual anomaly here, it's just a place that leads you to where something already documented happens.

That said, I agree with kaktus that the writing is solid and the dialogue strong. And watching the interviewer slowly lose his understanding of what's going on while the Sgt. is unaffected was pretty damned creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i know you tied this into the lethe events from elsewhere on the SCP site, but i'm also getting mad 3000 vibes from this article. possible link there too?

+1 for sure, love the atmosphere (no pun intended).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is super old school, but if I hadn't done what I did I like to imagine this is the sort of entry I would've written. The atmosphere throughout is consistent and dark, the dialogue is crisp, it doesn't overstay its welcome. This is really good.

item.title: "Old, Unhappy, Far-Off Things"
item.content:encoded:
This is a very late contest entry. I wasn't really sure what I wanted to do for this contest or if I was going to be able to enter, but I've had this draft sitting around for a long time, and I do think it fits the theme. I apologise for basically throwing something premade out there - I had a lot of stuff going on around the time of the contest - but I guess the readers will judge.

This was initially intended to be a doomsday contest entry; thanks to MrWrong for reviewing the draft, way back when, and ch00bakka and stormfallen for being on my team and being involved in brainstorming and discussions.

Also thanks to WrongJohnSilver for his blessing to use his team's worldbuilding.

Images my own.

Slot choices
5999
5001
5002
5005
5995
